title: Light therapy system and method
abstract: A light therapy system (12) for administering a controllable melanopic luminous exposure includes a lighting assembly (16) for producing a light output (68) having a configurable illuminance and color temperature. Based on a received target melanopic luminous exposure a controller (24) of the system constructs a suitable control schedule (71) for controlling parameter levels of the lighting assembly over time such that in total over a defined treatment period (78), the indicated target melanopic luminous exposure is delivered. In constructing the control schedule, the relative melanopic sensitivity of humans to light of different color temperatures is taken into account. The color temperature of the administered light is accordingly controlled such that after adjusting for color, the delivered luminous exposure conforms to the input exposure target.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS: 1. A light therapy sy stem ( 12) , comprising : a lighting assembly (16) operable to create a light output (68) having a controllable illuminance and color temperature; and a controller (24), operatively coupled to the lighting assembly (16), and adapted to: receive a data input (28) indicating a target melanopic luminous exposure for administration by the lighting assembly; retrieve from a data store (32) one or more melanopic weighting factors corresponding to a relative melanopic effect of different color temperatures of light; create, based on the melanopic weighting factors, a control schedule (71) for controlling the illuminance and color temperature values of the lighting assembly (16) over time such as to deliver in total over a defined treatment period (78) the target melanopic luminous exposure; and control the lighting assembly (16) in accordance with the control schedule (71). 2. A light therapy system (12) as claimed in claim 1, wherein the controller (24) is adapted to further receive color preference data indicating one or more preferred color temperature values, and to create the control schedule such that the color temperature of the light output, for at least a portion of the treatment period, has the preferred color temperature value. 3. A light therapy system (12) as claimed in claim 1 or 2, wherein the controller (24) is adapted to create the control schedule (71) based on a pre-stored schedule template (70), the template including a fixed temporal portion (74) and a configurable temporal portion (76), the controller being adapted to determine the configurable portion and wherein the two portions together are configured to deliver the target melanopic luminous exposure. 4. A light therapy system (12) as claimed in claim 3, wherein the controller (24) is adapted to create the control schedule (71) such that an average illuminance level during the configurable temporal portion (76) is higher than an average illuminance level during the fixed temporal portion (74). 5. A light therapy system (12) as claimed in claim 3 or 4, wherein the pre-stored schedule template includes a plurality of configurable temporal portions (90, 92), being separated by a plurality of intermediate fixed temporal portions (94, 96, 98). 6. A light therapy system (12) as claimed in any preceding claim, wherein the controller (24) is adapted to further retrieve from a data store (30) an indication of an upper and lower limit for the illuminance of the light output (68) during the treatment period (78) and an indication of an upper and lower limit for a duration (82) of at least a configurable temporal portion (76) of the treatment period, and to create at least a configurable temporal portion of the control schedule (71) in accordance with said values. 7. A light therapy system (12) as claimed in claim 6, further comprising a user input device (26) for receiving said data input (28) indicating the target melanopic luminous exposure and a user output device (26, 67), and wherein, in advance of receiving said target melanopic luminous exposure, the controller (24) is adapted to: determine, based upon said upper and lower limits for the illuminance and duration values, a maximum and minimum melanopic luminous exposure deliverable using the lighting assembly (16) for each of a set of light color temperatures; and control the user output device (26, 67) to communicate said determined upper and lower limits for each color temperature. 8. A light therapy system (12) as claimed in claim 6 or 7, wherein the controller (24) is further adapted to: determine, based on the upper and lower limits for duration and illuminance, a maximum and minimum value of at least one of the following parameters sufficient to deliver the target melanopic luminous exposure: color temperature of the light output (68) during at least a configurable temporal portion (76) of the control schedule (71), 

 duration (82) of at least a configurable temporal portion of the control schedule, and illuminance level of the light output during at least a controllable temporal portion of the control schedule; control the user output device (26, 67) to communicate the determined maximum and minimum values; receive from the user input device (26) a data input indicating a preferred value for one or more of said parameters; and create the control schedule (71) such that the light output (68), for at least a portion of the treatment period (78), is consistent with said preferred values. 9. A light therapy system (12) as claimed in any preceding claim, wherein the data store (30, 32, 34) is comprised by the controller (24) or wherein the data store is remote from the light therapy system (12). 10. A light therapy system (12) as claimed in any preceding claim, wherein the lighting assembly (16) comprises a plurality of light sources each having a respective spectral emission profile, and wherein control of the color temperature of the light output (68) comprises determining, based on pre-stored configuration data, light levels for each of said plurality of light sources. 11. A light therapy system (12) as claimed in any preceding claim further comprising an eye status sensor for detecting whether eyes of a user are open or closed, the sensor being operatively coupled with the controller (24), and the controller being adapted to adjust a duration (82) of, and/or the illuminance (84) and/or color temperature of, the light output (68) during at least a configurable temporal portion (76) of the control schedule (71) based on the readings from the sensor in order to ensure delivery of the target luminous exposure, and optionally wherein the controller (24) is adapted to calculate, based on readings from the eye status sensor over a defined sensing period, an aggregate time period for which a user's eyes have been closed, and to extend the duration (82) of at least a configurable temporal portion (76) of the control schedule by said aggregate time period. 12. A light therapy system (12) as claimed in any preceding claim, further comprising a presence sensor, operatively coupled with the controller (24) and arranged in use to detect the presence or absence of a user within a light path of the lighting assembly (16), and wherein the controller (24) is adapted to adjust a duration (82) of, and/or the illuminance (84) and/or color temperature of, the light output (68) during at least a configurable temporal portion (76) of the control schedule (71) based on readings from the sensor in order to ensure delivery of the target luminous exposure. 13. A light therapy system (12) as claimed in any preceding claim, further comprising a light level sensor, arranged to detect a light level proximal to a user, and wherein the controller (24) is adapted to adjust a duration (82) of, and/or the illuminance (84) and/or color temperature of, the light output (68) during at least a configurable temporal portion (76) of the control schedule (71) based on readings from the sensor, in order to ensure delivery of the target luminous exposure, and optionally wherein the light level sensor is arranged proximal to a user's eye or eyes. 14. A light therapy system (12) as claimed in any preceding claim, wherein the controller (24) is further adapted to: retrieve from a data store (62) an indication of an age of a user; retrieve from a data store (62) a set of age weighting factors, being based on average sensitivity to light of users of different ages; and adjust a duration (82) of, and/or the illuminance (84) and/or color temperature of, the light output (68) during at least a configurable temporal portion (76) of the control schedule (71) based on the retrieved age and retrieved weighting factors in order to ensure delivery of the target luminous exposure. 15. A light therapy method comprising controlling a lighting assembly (16) to deliver a target melanopic luminous exposure, the lighting assembly being operable to create a light output (68) having a controllable illuminance and color temperature, and the method comprising: receiving a data input (28) indicating a target melanopic luminous exposure for administration by the lighting assembly; retrieving from a data store (32) one or more melanopic weighting factors 

 corresponding to a relative melanopic effect of different color temperatures of light; creating, based on the melanopic weighting factors, a control schedule (71) for controlling the illuminance and color temperature values of the lighting assembly (16) over time such as to deliver in total over a defined treatment period (78) the target melanopic luminous exposure; and controlling the lighting assembly (16) in accordance with the control schedule.
